summary: All three requirements are substantially implemented. REQ-1 is fully met with 12 prefixes and 12 suffixes each carrying stat modifiers, and a generateMonster() function combining prefix+base+suffix stats. REQ-2 is implemented with an additive XP formula using equal STAT_BONUS_PER_POINT constants. REQ-3 is implemented with DungeonSelectScreen generating monsters and DungeonRunScreen accepting and using them. One minor concern: the generated monster name follows 'Prefix BaseName Suffix' (three-part) rather than the specified 'Prefix Suffix' (two-part) pattern, though the spec examples show 'Prefix Suffix' like 'Infernal Titan' and 'Ancient Elder God'.

missing_requirements.requirement: Monster names displayed in combat follow the 'Prefix Suffix' pattern (e.g., 'Ancient Elder God') — two-part name without a base template name in between.
missing_requirements.reason: The diff shows monsters.ts generates names as 'Prefix BaseName Suffix' (three-part, e.g., 'Ancient Goblin Elder God') rather than the specified 'Prefix Suffix' two-part pattern. The frontend-file-summaries.txt explicitly states the name format is 'Prefix BaseName Suffix'.

hallucinated_features.description: A base monster template pool (MONSTER_TEMPLATES) with 10 named monster types (Goblin, Skeleton, Orc, Troll, Wraith, Vampire, etc.) is incorporated into the name generation as a middle segment, creating a three-part naming system not specified in the requirements.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request specifies a prefix+suffix system producing 'Prefix Suffix' names. The implementation adds a third base template component to the name, which was not requested.
